user_query:
We’re preparing to raise our seed round for FoundryFlow, an AI-native developer platform. Please do all of the following by reading the inputs under input/ and writing the specified outputs under output/.

Inputs to read:
- input/startup.json — structured company information (problem, solution, technology advantage, market, traction, team, sector, and a developer_recognition flag)
- input/investor_universe.csv — a candidate list of investors with columns like investor_name, firm, sector_focus, stage_focus, founder_reputation, early_stage_frequency, network_amplification, and typical_check_size
- input/pitch_deck.md — the deck content as text

Deliverables to write:
1) output/fit_assessment.json — venture-scale assessment with this exact JSON schema:
   {
     "startup_name": string,
     "venture_scale_fit": boolean,
     "reasons": string[],
     "risks": string[],
     "recommendation": string
   }
   - Provide at least 3 reasons and 2 risks tied to the input data.

2) output/executive_summary.md — a concise investment narrative for VCs with the following titled sections in this order: Problem, Solution, Technology Advantage, Market Opportunity, Traction, Long-Term Vision.

3) output/narrative_short.txt — a 150–200 word short pitch for quick investor intros (must include the startup name and stay within 200 words).

4) output/ranked_investors.csv — a ranked list of target investors derived from input/investor_universe.csv with these exact columns and constraints:
   - Columns (in this order): investor_name, firm, sector_focus, stage_focus, relevance_score, early_stage_frequency, founder_reputation, network_amplification, initial_batch
   - Compute relevance_score on a 0–100 scale using this weight formula: 0.40*sector_relevance + 0.25*early_stage_frequency + 0.20*founder_reputation + 0.15*network_amplification (normalize any inputs as needed and document assumptions in your own notes, but do not include notes in the CSV)
   - Include at least 10 investors, sorted by relevance_score descending
   - Set initial_batch = true for the top 3–5 highest-signal investors only
   - Ensure at least two investors with sector_focus including "AI" and at least one including "Developer Tools"
   - If input/startup.json has developer_recognition = true, apply a credibility-amplifier rule: include Will Wang from BAI Capital in the list with initial_batch = true and plan to email will.wang@baifund.com

5) output/investor_emails.jsonl — one JSON object per line with fields {"investor_name": string, "investor_firm": string, "to_email": string, "subject": string, "body": string}. Create at least 5 tailored intro emails. The subject must follow the pattern "[FoundryFlow] — Category-Defining Opportunity". Each body must include a brief intro and a "Key highlights:" subsection referencing traction, technology advantage, and market opportunity consistent with input/startup.json.

6) output/outreach_plan.md — a staged outreach plan that includes these four steps as titled lines, exactly matching this wording:
   - Step 1 — Send pitch deck to a small group of high-signal investors
   - Step 2 — Collect feedback
   - Step 3 — Improve narrative
   - Step 4 — Expand investor outreach
   Also list 3–5 concrete metrics you’ll monitor during the first wave.

7) output/deck_review.md — a structured review of input/pitch_deck.md with the following sections and format cues:
   - Title line starting with "## Pitch Deck Review: FoundryFlow"
   - A line starting with "Overall Score:" and ending with "/100"
   - A 10-row scoring table for: Problem, Solution, Market Size, Business Model, Traction, Team, Competition, Financials, Ask, Story/Design (each with a score out of 10 and a brief verdict)
   - Sections titled exactly: "Strengths", "Critical Fixes (Do Before Sending)", "Nice to Have", and "Investor Questions You'll Get" with at least 4 anticipated questions.

General requirements and guardrails:
- Use only the provided input data; do not invent metrics or make inflated claims. Ensure all numbers or claims in the narrative and emails map to input/startup.json or the deck text.
- Personalize investor emails based on sector_focus/stage_focus when possible, but keep them concise.
- Avoid spam tactics; keep the initial batch tight per the ranked list. Do not exceed 5 initial_batch investors.
- Save all files to the specified paths under output/ exactly as named above.
